domain: Inca tampu way-station network (Andean Inca road system; one-day-walking-interval tampu stations + chasqui-wasi relay houses every 2.5 km; modular standardized structures supporting 1200-mile 5-day relay communication)
specific_mechanism: Two-level standardized modular way-station hierarchy: major tampu at long intervals + chasqui-wasi at short intervals; each level provides differentiated function (rest/supplies vs runner switching).
llm_application: TAMPU-CHASQUI-ADAPTER: a two-level adapter insertion scheme for transformer LLMs. Major adapters (TAMPU) inserted every K layers (e.g., K=4) with full PEFT capability (rank-r LoRA, full Q/K/V/MLP adaptation). Minor adapters (CHASQUI-WASI) inserted EVERY LAYER as lightweight rank-1 sub-adapters (e.g., LoRA rank=1 on attention out_proj only). The two levels have DIFFERENT parameter budgets + DIFFERENT functions (heavy adaptation vs frequent micro-corrections). Differs from prior LoRA (single uniform rank, every-layer) and MoA (mixture but same-position) by FIXED TWO-LEVEL SPACING with HETEROGENEOUS adapter functions per level.
novelty_claim: Standard LoRA uses uniform-rank every-layer insertion. AdaLoRA dynamically allocates rank. MoA mixes adapter types at same positions. LLM-Adapters is uniform adapter family. Mixture-of-Adapters parallel adapters per layer. TAMPU-CHASQUI-ADAPTER differs by (a) TWO-LEVEL fixed-spacing scheme, (b) HETEROGENEOUS function per level (major full PEFT vs minor rank-1 micro), (c) DIFFERENT PARAMETER BUDGETS per level. The hierarchical spacing-vs-function combination is novel.
content_words: tampu major every K-layers + chasqui-wasi minor every-layer

llm_side: tampu major every K-layers + chasqui-wasi minor every-layer
